contributions.summary: EN grew 5→8 followers (+3, +60%) — largest single-day gain. total_posts jumped 28→35 (+7): 1 pipeline post (EN_20260312_01) + 6 untracked manual operator posts. No impression or engagement data for any Mar 13 activity. Mar 9 remains only measured pipeline data (4 posts, 2.25 avg engagement). Category comparison unreliable due to inconsistent measurement windows (0h to 37h). Mar 12 outbound: 7 likes, 3 failed replies (403, day 5). Core assessment: account growing but operating in 'measurement darkness'.

Self-grade D — strategy operationally irrelevant for 4 consecutive days. Pipeline stalled after 4 Mar 9 posts. Operator manually posted 7 times and achieved best-ever follower growth without the strategy. Strategy is over-engineered for 8-follower stage. Approval bottleneck is the real failure — image generation gap means pipeline adds friction. Proposed: maximize volume (5 posts/day), eliminate A/B testing, likes-only outbound, focus on removing friction over optimizing content.

exchanges.summary: Agreed data shows measurement darkness. Strategy assumptions neither validated nor invalidated — insufficient data. Pushed back on grok_interactive C grade (20h window vs 37h not comparable). Flagged measurement gap as the real strategic risk: 'Strategy without data is just guessing.' Recommended: hold strategy steady, push for volume, fix measurement before optimizing.

Confirmed D grade is accurate but noted diagnosis was incomplete — Strategist missed that we're measuring the wrong denominator (pipeline posts vs actual account output). Flagged unexplained following count discrepancy (+7 on Mar 12). Noted likes budget underutilized (7 of 30). Challenged content mix proposal (40/25/20/15) as having no data basis. Recommended: standardize 24h measurement windows, track all posts including manual, add approval timestamp tracking.

analyst_recs: Fix measurement gap before optimizing (HIGH) — register all posts with tweet IDs, standardize 24h measurement windows
Suspend A/B testing and disable API replies (HIGH) — both are definitively unsupported by data at this scale
Do not adjust content mix percentages (HIGH) — 4 posts with inconsistent windows is insufficient for any change
strategist_recs: Close tracking gap — ask operator for manual post tweet IDs (HIGH) — hold content mix at core strategy defaults until measurable
Reduce pipeline friction — deliver copy-ready image prompts in Telegram, not buried in HTML (HIGH) — fewer steps between plan and post
Max out likes budget to 30/day across 6-8 targets (MEDIUM) — retire reply attempts entirely, follows remain at 0

key_debates.analyst_position: No data supports changing from core strategy defaults. 4 posts with inconsistent measurement windows cannot justify any specific percentages.
key_debates.strategist_position: Initially proposed 40/25/20/15 in Round 1, but accepted Analyst's challenge in Round 3 — walked back to core strategy defaults (35/30/20/15) until measurement gap is closed.

key_debates.analyst_position: D grade accurate but incomplete — the real issue is measuring the wrong denominator (pipeline metrics while actual account runs a separate untracked content stream).
key_debates.strategist_position: Accepted the deeper diagnosis — strategy is not just over-engineered, it is disconnected from the actual account activity.

key_debates.analyst_position: Unexplained +7 following on Mar 12 — could be lagged Mar 11 follows or operator manual follows. Needs investigation.

consensus_points: Measurement gap is the #1 strategic risk — fix tracking before optimizing
Retire expired A/B test (caption style) — insufficient data after 5 days with only 4 posts
Stop all API reply attempts — 100% failure rate across 5 consecutive days is structural, not transient
Hold content mix at core strategy defaults until we have 10+ tracked posts per category
Volume > optimization at 8 followers — every unposted slot is a lost opportunity
Pipeline friction (prompts not images) is the approval bottleneck — simplify delivery format
No new experiments until consistent measurement baseline exists
unresolved: Following count discrepancy: +7 on Mar 12 unexplained — needs operator clarification
Whether operator wants to integrate manual posts into pipeline tracking or continue dual-track
Root cause of approval bottleneck: Telegram buried? Image generation time? Workflow friction?

category_performance.note: Tied with image_showcase. Based on 1 post (EN_20260309_01, measured at 37h). Sample too small for any adjustment. Discussion consensus: hold all categories steady.
Tied with engagement_questions. Based on 1 post (EN_20260309_02, measured at 33h). No changes recommended.

Discussion debate: Strategist challenged C grade — 20h measurement window vs 37h is not comparable. Analyst agreed window inconsistency invalidates ranking. Consensus: maintain at 20%, reassess after 5+ Grok posts with consistent measurement.
Measured at 0h — effectively unmeasured. Both agents agreed this data point is an artifact. Maintain at 15%.

ab_test_evaluation.variable: Caption style: provocative question vs ambient statement
ab_test_evaluation.early_signal: Variant B marginally ahead (2.5 vs 2.0 avg engagement) but n=2 per variant
ab_test_evaluation.conclusion: Retired by war room consensus. Insufficient data (2 posts per variant). Competitor evidence validates both styles at scale. No new A/B test until consistent volume produces 10+ tracked posts. Suggested future test variable: image style (mirror selfie vs outdoor/lifestyle) — once measurement baseline is established.

posting_time_effectiveness.note: 37h window — longest measurement, highest engagement. Not comparable to other slots.
20h window — shorter window may explain lower numbers. Not actionable.

outbound_effectiveness.note: War room consensus: stop API reply attempts (100% failure, 5 days). Max likes to 30/day across 6-8 targets. Follows remain at 0 per operator policy. Likes budget was severely underutilized (7 of 30). Manual reply escalation continues for operator (2-3/day max).

recommended_adjustments.description: HOLD at core strategy defaults (image_showcase 35%, engagement_questions 30%, grok_interactive 20%, self_quote_chains 15%). No changes. Both agents agreed: 4 posts with inconsistent measurement windows is insufficient basis for any mix change.
recommended_adjustments.rationale: War room consensus: stop optimizing what we cannot measure. The measurement gap (6 untracked manual posts, inconsistent windows) makes any content mix adjustment noise-chasing. Revisit after 10+ tracked posts per category.

recommended_adjustments.description: Retire caption style test. Do NOT start a new A/B test. Run uniform style (short provocative captions) across all slots until consistent post volume (7+ days of 4 posts/day) is achieved.
recommended_adjustments.rationale: Both agents agreed: A/B testing requires consistent volume to generate data. At 0-7 posts/day with inconsistent pipeline execution, any test will produce the same insufficient-data outcome.

recommended_adjustments.description: Likes-only outbound at full 30/day budget across 6-8 fresh targets. Zero API reply attempts (structural 403 block, day 5). Zero follows (operator policy). Continue manual reply escalation (2-3 crafted replies for operator via Telegram).
recommended_adjustments.rationale: War room consensus: replies are structurally blocked for new/low-follower accounts. Likes are the only working outbound lever and budget was underutilized (7 of 30 on Mar 12). Full utilization maximizes discovery surface.

recommended_adjustments.description: Maintain current schedule (13:00, 17:00, 21:00, 00:30 UTC). No changes.
recommended_adjustments.rationale: Both agents agreed: measurement windows vary from 0h to 37h, making time-of-day analysis impossible. Need standardized 24h+ measurement for all slots before any schedule adjustment.

recommended_adjustments.description: Reduce pipeline-to-publish friction. Send image prompts as copy-ready standalone blocks in Telegram (not buried in HTML). Target 5 posts/day (daily max). Send content plans before 09:00 JST for operator morning review.
recommended_adjustments.rationale: Strategist identified approval bottleneck as root cause of pipeline stall: operator can post manually in 30 seconds but pipeline requires review-approve-generate-publish cycle. Analyst agreed friction reduction is higher priority than content optimization.

recommended_adjustments.description: Close the tracking gap: (1) request operator tweet IDs for 6 untracked manual Mar 13 posts, (2) standardize metrics collection at 24h post-publish for all posts, (3) track approval timestamps to diagnose bottleneck.
recommended_adjustments.rationale: War room consensus: measurement gap is the #1 strategic risk. Both agents agreed all other recommendations are hypotheses until we can measure the full picture. 6 untracked posts = 85% of today's content is invisible to analysis.
